libcameraservice in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.x before 5.1.1 LMY49H, and 6.x before 2016-03-01 does not require use of the ICameraService::dump method for a camera service dump, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application that directly dumps, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26265403. This issue is rated as High because it could be used to gain privileges via a crafted application that directly dumps. Product: Android. Versions: 4.4.4, 5.0.2, 5.1.1, 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2

libFLAC/stream_decoder.c in mediaserver in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-05-01 does not prevent free operations on uninitialized memory, which allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (heap memory corruption) via a crafted media file, aka internal bug 27211885. NOTE: this issue was SPLIT from internal bug 27211885 because it has a different set of affected versions. This issue exists because of an incorrect fix for CVE-2016-5773. Android ID: A-35219737.

media/libmedia/IDrm.cpp in mediaserver in Android 6.x before 2016-04-01 does not initialize a certain key-request data structure, which allows attackers to obtain sensitive information from process memory, and consequently bypass an unspecified protection mechanism, via unspecified vectors, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26323455. This issue is rated as Moderate because it could be used to access sensitive data without permission. Product: Android. Versions: 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1. Android ID: A-34749392.

In smp_proc_enc_info of smp_act.cc, there is a possible out of bounds read due to a missing bounds check. This could lead to remote information disclosure over Bluetooth with no additional execution privileges needed. User interaction is not needed for exploitation. Use-after-free vulnerability in the Linux kernel before 3.3.6, when huge pages are enabled, allows local users to cause a denial of service (system crash) or possibly gain privileges by interacting with a hugetlbfs filesystem, as demonstrated by a umount operation that triggers improper handling of quota data.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2011-4131.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2011-4131.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2011-4131.

libs/binder/IMemory.cpp in the IMemory Native Interface in Android 4.x before 4.4.4, 5.0.x before 5.0.2, 5.1.x before 5.1.1, and 6.x before 2016-04-01 does not properly consider the heap size, which allows attackers to gain privileges via a crafted application, as demonstrated by obtaining Signature or SignatureOrSystem access, aka internal bug 26877992. This issue is rated as Low due to the possibility of an attack vector that requires a local user to gain privileges, which is not common for this interface. Product: Android. This issue is rated as Moderate because it could be used to gain privileges or cause a denial of service. Product: Android. Versions: 5.0.2, 5.1.1, 6.0, 6.0.1, 7.0, 7.1.1, 7.1.2. A malicious webview could install long-lived unload handlers that re-use an incognito BrowserContext that is queued for destruction in versions of Oxide before 1.18.3. This could cause a denial of service if the data directory is on an untrusted file system. Android users are unaffected. Fixed in 1.18.3.

An out-of-bounds memory access issue was found in Quick Emulator (QEMU) before 1.7.2 in the VNC display driver. This flaw could occur while refreshing the VNC display surface area in the 'vnc_refresh_server_surface'. A user inside a guest could use this flaw to crash the QEMU process. It is also possible to crash the QEMU process by sending crafted VNC packets to the guest. It can be mitigated by limiting the size of the VNC display surface area in the guest. This is not a vulnerability in QEMU itself but in the VNC display driver. QEMU does not use the VNC display driver by default. However, if a guest uses the VNC display driver, it is affected. Fixed in QEMU 1.7.2.

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). A remote XML entity can be included with the XML_PARSE_DTD flag, allowing remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags. This is related to "XML External Entity (XXE) Processing" in CVE-2013-7345. Fixed in libxml2 2.9.2. A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). A similar issue is identified in libxslt before 1.1.28. In libxslt, an attacker able to supply crafted XML data to an application that accepts XML input could use this flaw to crash the application or possibly execute arbitrary code. Both libxml2 and libxslt are used in Red Hat Enterprise MRG 2.3, Red Hat Enterprise Linux 6.5, and Red Hat Enterprise Linux 7.0. libxml2 is used in Red Hat Enterprise MRG 2

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). A remote XML entity can be included with the default parser flags by calling the XML parser interface directly. This is different from the SAX interface, which requires an application to request entity substitution. In general, we recommend that client applications use the SAX interface for XML parsing, because it is more powerful and flexible. In cases where the client must use the parser interface directly, we recommend that applications request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, and default DTD attributes be disabled. An issue was discovered in the Linux kernel before 5.0.9. There is a use-after-free upon attempted read access to /proc/ioports after the ipmi_si module is removed, related to drivers/char/ipmi/ipmi_si_intf.c, drivers/char/ipmi/ipmi_si_mem_io.c, and drivers/char/ipmi/ipmi_si_port_io.c. This occurs because the driver's ->remove() function does not properly NULL out the ->data pointer, and so ipmi_si_intf_io_remove() calls ipmi_si_mem_io_remove() or ipmi_si_port_io_remove() with a NULL ->intf pointer. A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). A similar issue exists in Expat, a parser used in Google Chrome before 13.0.782.215. Fixed in libxml2 2.9.1 and libxml2 2.9.2. Fixed in Expat 2.2.1 and 2.2.2. In libsndfile before 1.0.28, an error in the *header_read()* function (common.c) when handling ID3 tags can be exploited to cause a stack-based buffer overflow via a specially crafted FLAC file. This is related to incorrect validation of the size of the ID3 tag in *header_read()*. A remote attacker, who is able to provide a specially crafted FLAC file to a *sf_open()* call, may be able to crash the application or possibly execute arbitrary code. It is also possible that an attacker may be able to cause a denial of service by providing a specially crafted FLAC file to *sf_read_short()*; this may lead to a crash or possibly an out-of-bounds write. A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). A remote XML entity can be included with the default parser flags if the entity is (1) in the DTD, (2) referenced from an external DTD subset, or (3) used as default DTD attributes. In the first case, the entity must be declared with a public ID, a system ID, or be in the default DTD subset. In the second case, the entity must be properly declared in the external DTD subset. A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). A remote XML entity can be included with the SAX2, DOM, or the default parser in Expat (aka libexpat) through ExpatParser_ParseXml with a crafted XML input file. In the case of a DOM document, a similar attack can be performed with the default parser in libxml2 through xmlReadFile. ARM mbed TLS before 2.1.11, before 2.7.2, and before 2.8.0 has a buffer over-read in ssl_parse_server_key_exchange() that could cause a crash on invalid input. This is related to ssl_parse_client_hello() and ssl_parse_server_key_exchange() in library/ssl_srv.c. Remote attackers could leverage this vulnerability to cause a denial of service via crafted handshake messages. The issue is in library/ssl_srv.c, functions ssl_parse_client_hello() and ssl_parse_server_key_exchange(). The fix is to check the return value of mbedtls_asn1_get_bitstring() and mbedtls_asn1_get_alg() in ssl_parse_client_hello() and ssl_parse_server_key_exchange() to verify the ASN.1 parsing was successful.

Race condition in the IP implementation in the Linux kernel before 3.0 might allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (slab corruption and system crash) by sending packets to an application that sets socket options during the handling of network traffic. This is related to net/ipv4/ip_sockglue.c, net/ipv6/ip6_sockglue.c, and net/netfilter/nf_conntrack_reasm.c. It can occur with any application that sets socket options during the handling of network traffic. It does not occur with TCP, because the TCP implementation does not set any socket options during the handling of network traffic.

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). A similar issue exists in libxslt before 1.1.28. In libxslt, an attacker able to supply crafted XML data to an application that accepts XML input could use this flaw to crash the application or possibly execute arbitrary code. Both libxml2 and libxslt are used by OpenOffice and Thunderbird. Fixed in libxml2 2.9.2 and libxslt 1.1.28. Fixed in OpenOffice 3.3 and 3.4 Bet

Race condition in the IP implementation in the Linux kernel before 3.0 might allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (slab corruption and system crash) by sending packets to an application that sets socket options during the handling of network traffic. For example, a remote attacker could use this flaw to crash a web server by sending a crafted packet to the server that sets socket options during the handling of an HTTP request. This issue is rated as Critical due to the possibility of remote denial of service. It could be argued that it has a higher severity than CVE-2010-5475 because it requires an uncommon configuration. Linux kernel versions 3.0.10 onwards are not affected. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2010-5475. It is also possible that an attacker could use a different vulnerability, a different flaw, or a different approach t

Race condition in the IP implementation in the Linux kernel before 3.0 might allow remote attackers to cause a denial of service (slab corruption and system crash) by sending packets to an application that sets socket options during the handling of network traffic. For example, a remote attacker could use this flaw to crash a web server by sending a crafted packet to the server that sets socket options during the handling of an HTTP request. This issue is rated as Critical due to the possibility of remote denial of service. It can be mitigated by ensuring that the socket options are set before the socket is used. Also, an application that uses the IP_HDRINCL socket option should ensure that it sets the IP_HDRINCL socket option before the socket is used. The Linux kernel before 3.3.2 does not have this issue. Fixed in Linux kernel version 3.0.34. Bug is also present in 3.1.x

A flaw in libxml2 allows remote XML entity inclusion with default parser flags (i.e., when the caller did not request entity substitution, DTD validation, external DTD subset loading, or default DTD attributes). Depending on the context, this may expose a higher-risk attack surface in libxml2 not usually reachable with default parser flags, and expose content from local files, HTTP, or FTP servers (which might be otherwise unreachable). A remote XML entity can be included with the default parser flags if the entity is defined in an external DTD subset, or in the DTD passed to the xmlSAX2SetDTD function. In that case, it is also necessary to set the XML_PARSE_DTDLOAD flag. In case of an external DTD subset, the name of the external subset must be correct. ImageMagick before 7.0.8-50 has a memory leak vulnerability in the function ReadVIFFImage in coders/viff.c. Remote attackers could leverage this vulnerability to cause a denial of service by sending a crafted file. The attack vector for this vulnerability is an invalid file size in a crafted file. This issue is rated as High due to the possibility of remote denial of service. ImageMagick 7.0.8-50 has been fixed upstream. The fix will be available in ImageMagick 7.0.9-50 and ImageMagick 6.9.9-50. Users are encouraged to upgrade to these versions.

Das U-Boot versions 2016.09 through 2019.07-rc4 can memset() too much data while reading a crafted ext4 filesystem, which results in a stack buffer overflow and likely code execution. This occurs because ext4_fill_super() in fs/ext4/super.c does not properly validate the size of the super block. A user inside a container could use this to gain privileges. This is fixed in 2019.07-rc5. NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2019-5783. ext4_fill_super() is only used when ext4 support is built in. ext4 filesystems are not commonly used. Das U-Boot versions 2016.09 through 2019.07-rc4 can memset() too much data while reading a crafted ext4 filesystem, which results in a stack buffer overflow and likely code execution. This occurs because ext4_fill_super() in fs/ext4/super.c does not properly validate the size of the super block. A user inside a chrooted environment, who can control the size of the ext4 filesystem, could use this flaw to overflow the stack and execute arbitrary code. This is related to fs/ext4/extents.c, fs/ext4/extent_cache.c, and drivers/block/paride/pf.c. It can also be exploited in conjunction with CVE-2019-5736. In libsndfile before 1.0.28, an error in the *header_read()* function (common.c) when handling ID3 tags can be exploited to cause a stack-based buffer overflow via a specially crafted FLAC file. This is related to incorrect validation of the size of the ID3 tag in *header_read()*. A remote attacker could use this flaw to cause a denial of service via a specially crafted FLAC file. It is also possible that an attacker could use this flaw with a specially crafted Ogg Vorbis file to cause a stack-based buffer overflow. Both of these attacks are believed to be exploitable via the file format used in the Android media framework (e.g., an attack against a media player). Fixed in version 1.0.28. The TCP stack in the Linux kernel through 4.10.6 mishandles the SCM_TIMESTAMPING_OPT_STATS feature, which allows local users to obtain sensitive information from the kernel's internal socket data structures or cause a denial of service (out-of-bounds read) via crafted system calls, related to net/core/skbuff.c and net/socket.c.  NOTE: this vulnerability exists because of an incomplete fix for CVE-2016-8890.  In other words, CVE-2016-8890 is not a vulnerability in itself, but it does make it easier for an attacker to trigger it.  Also, Linux kernel versions 4.9.x, 4.10.x, and 4.11.x are also affected.  Fixed in Linux kernel 4.11.12.  Also, in 4.

An issue was discovered in the Linux kernel before 5.0.9. There is a use-after-free upon attempted read access to /proc/ioports after the ipmi_si module is removed, related to drivers/char/ipmi/ipmi_si_intf.c, drivers/char/ipmi/ipmi_si_mem_io.c, and drivers/char/ipmi/ipmi_si_port_io.c. This occurs because the driver's ->remove() function does not properly NULL out the ->data pointer, and so ipmi_si_intf_io_destroy() does not properly handle the case where ->data is NULL. NOTE: this is not a vulnerability in the Linux kernel before 5.0.9. It was introduced in the Linux kernel before 5.0.10. The ipmi_si module is not enabled in the Linux kernel by default. The Linux kernel before 5.1-rc5 allows page->_refcount reference count overflow, with resultant use-after-free issues, if about 140 GiB of RAM exists. This is related to fs/fuse/dev.c, fs/pipe.c, fs/splice.c, include/linux/mm.h, include/linux/pipe_fs_i.h, kernel/trace/trace.c, mm/gup.c, and mm/hugetlb.c. It can occur with FUSE requests. It can also occur with untrusted user-space code that leverages page->_refcount overflow to cause a denial of service. It can be prevented entirely by using pgoff_t instead of unsigned long for page offsets. Fixed in Linux kernel 5.1-rc5.1. (Fixing this affects the FUSE_INIT_IN_PROCESS feature.) Fixed in Linux kerne

In ImageMagick 7.x before 7.0.8-41 and 6.x before 6.9.10-41, there is a use after free vulnerability in the UnmapBlob function that allows an attacker to cause a denial of service by sending a crafted file. This occurs because UnmapBlob is used to clear sensitive data from memory after it has been decrypted but fails to check for the presence of a crypt context. An attacker would need to be able to supply a crafted file to exploit this vulnerability. The file could contain a crafted PixelCache structure that points to a memory region that does not belong to the PixelCache structure, leading to a segmentation fault. A successful exploitation of this vulnerability could allow an attacker to cause a denial of service in ImageMagick. It is also possible that an attacker could use this vulnerability to crash the application. This is similar to CVE-2019-11

sf-pcapng.c in libpcap before 1.9.1 does not properly validate the PHB header length before allocating memory. This may cause a denial of service (application crash) by opening a crafted file. It is also possible that an attacker may be able to use this vulnerability to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) by opening a crafted file. This vulnerability is related to the pcapng_read_packet_header function in sf-pcapng.c. Fixed in libpcap 1.9.1 and libpcap-dev 1.9.1+20160701. An attacker may be able to use this vulnerability to cause a denial of service (application crash) via a crafted file. This vulnerability is related to the pcapng_read_packet_header function in sf-pcapng.c. Fixed in libpcap 1.9.1 and libpcap

In ImageMagick before 7.0.8-50, some memory leaks exist in DecodeImage in coders/pcd.c. Remote attackers could leverage this vulnerability to cause a denial of service by sending a crafted PCD image file. The issue is in ReadPCDChannel in coders/pcd.c. The channel data is not properly freed. A crafted PCD file could trigger a segmentation fault during processing. This is due to a missing *channel->depth++*. A *channel->depth* value of zero indicates that the channel data is grayscale. This is not handled properly in WritePSDChannel in coders/psd.c. A *channel->depth* value of zero will lead to a divide-by-zero error. This will cause ImageMagick to crash. This affects ImageMagick before 7.0.8-50 and 6.9.x before 6.9.
